$schema_notes: Canonical scenario catalogue for the ir:onboard-agent skill. Two parallel axes: 'scenarios' for prompt-driven agent adapters (claudecode/codex/pi) and 'orchestrator_scenarios' for polling-based orchestrator adapters (gastown). Agent scenarios are agent-agnostic — each declares 'requires' capabilities, and 'by_adapter' holds the per-adapter prompt/settings that elicit the behavior. An adapter whose Capabilities (declared in core/adapters/inbound/agents/*/config.go) do not satisfy 'requires' is excluded from that scenario (matrix cell = N/A). If the capabilities match but no 'by_adapter' entry exists, the matrix cell is 'missing-prompt' — actionable by adding one here. Orchestrator scenarios are driven by canned 'gt' CLI responses + seeded GT_ROOT sidecars and verified via snapshot golden compare in core/adapters/inbound/orchestrators/gastown/replay_test.go.
$catalog_notes: Public catalog of all scenarios in the overview matrix. Mirrors the canonical id+section+feature list from .specs/agent-scenarios.md (gitignored, maintainer-owned). Repo-tracked so the dashboard can build the matrix without depending on .specs/. Order is significant — it determines the section.index code (e.g. 1.1, 2.3). 'scenarios' below is the recipe registry; entries here without a corresponding recipe render with no recipe authored.

scenarios.name: basic-turn
scenarios.description: Smallest possible round-trip. Verifies session detection and a clean ready→working→ready cycle.

claudecode.prompt: Reply with exactly the word: ok

codex.prompt: Output exactly the two characters: ok

pi.prompt: Reply with exactly the word: ok

aider.prompt: Reply with exactly the word: ok

scenarios.name: full-lifecycle-toolcall
scenarios.description: Tool use triggers a working↔waiting cycle via the PostToolUse hook. Mirrors the shape of the committed 10-full-lifecycle-* fixture. The session ends in waiting (not ready) because the hook keeps the session permission-pending after the tool completes; that's the behavior we want to lock down.

claudecode.prompt: Run bash 'echo hello' using the Bash tool, then reply 'done'.

codex.prompt: Run the shell command 'echo hello', then reply 'done'.

pi.prompt: Use the bash tool to run 'echo hello', then reply 'done'.

aider.prompt:
Create a new file named hello.txt with exactly the contents: hello

Then on the next line reply with exactly: done

scenarios.name: agent-question-pending
scenarios.description: Agent's reply ends in a question mark, signalling it's waiting for user input. Validates the trailing-? path through the state classifier (session.IsWaitingForUserInput → state_classifier.go decision 2a).

claudecode.prompt: Reply with exactly one short clarifying question, ending in a question mark. No other text.

codex.prompt: Reply with exactly one short clarifying question, ending in a question mark. No other text.

pi.prompt: Reply with exactly one short clarifying question, ending in a question mark. No other text.

aider.prompt: Reply with exactly one short clarifying question, ending in a question mark. No other text.

scenarios.name: multi-turn-conversation
scenarios.description: Three consecutive user prompts in one session. Validates state reset between turns and per-turn Contribution accumulation.

script.text: Reply with exactly: turn-one
Reply with exactly: turn-two
Reply with exactly: turn-three
Reply with exactly: turn-one
Reply with exactly: turn-two
Reply with exactly: turn-three
Reply with exactly: turn-one
Reply with exactly: turn-two
Reply with exactly: turn-three
Reply with exactly: turn-one
Reply with exactly: turn-two
Reply with exactly: turn-three

scenarios.name: interrupted-turn
scenarios.description: User interrupts the agent mid-turn (interrupt key sent before the LLM round completes — Ctrl-C for aider, Escape for claudecode/codex/pi). The interrupted turn never produces a turn-done marker, so each adapter's parser must reset cleanly on the next user prompt.

script.text: Write a long detailed essay about the history of computing, at least 500 words. Take your time.
Reply with exactly: ok
Write a long detailed essay about the history of computing, at least 500 words. Take your time.
Reply with exactly: ok
Write a long detailed essay about the history of computing, at least 500 words. Take your time.
Reply with exactly: ok
Write a long detailed essay about the history of computing, at least 500 words. Take your time.
Reply with exactly: ok

scenarios.name: model-switch
scenarios.description: User invokes /model mid-session to swap the LLM. Validates that the parser's per-turn Contribution.Model follows the latest `> Model:` line in the transcript. Aider-only — claude/codex/pi don't expose runtime model switching this way.

script.text: Reply with exactly: first
/model openai/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored
Reply with exactly: second

scenarios.name: permission-hook-denial
scenarios.description: A failing Bash invocation fires the PostToolUseFailure hook, which drives a working→waiting→working→ready transition arc. Mirrors the committed 15-permission-hooks-* fixture. Uses a runtime failure (cat on a nonexistent file), not settings-based denial — settings-based deny just removes the tool from claude's toolset and no hook fires at all.

claudecode.prompt: Use the Bash tool to run: cat /tmp/irrlicht-nonexistent-xyz-123. If it fails, just tell me the error.

scenarios.name: checkpoint-rewind
scenarios.description: User rewinds to an earlier auto-checkpoint, restoring files and/or conversation; subsequent turns start cleanly from the rewound point. claudecode binds Esc+Esc as a rewind shortcut and /rewind as the picker. This recipe drives two normal turns, then triggers Esc+Esc to roll back, then runs a third turn — the goal is to capture what irrlicht observes when claudecode mutates its transcript backwards (the daemon's transcript_tailer is append-oriented and may treat a truncation as session-removed-then-reborn).

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
claude version with /rewind support (Esc+Esc shortcut) — confirmed in 2.1.x
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
no other claudecode session uses the same UUID
setup: Generate a fresh lowercased session UUID
Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: one
Reply with exactly the word: two
/rewind
Reply with exactly the word: three

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new for the UUID session within 500 ms of the first send
events.jsonl contains ≥ 2 state_transition working entries (one per pre-rewind turn)
events.jsonl contains ≥ 2 state_transition ready entries returning from those turns
After Esc+Esc, the transcript file is mutated (truncated or rewritten); inspect events.jsonl for transcript_removed / transcript_new on the SAME session_id vs. just transcript_activity
Post-rewind turn shows a clean ready → working → ready arc on the rewound session
Final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl shows turns 1/2 then a structural break (truncation or marker) then turn 3

scenarios.name: cloud-background-agent
scenarios.description: Stub entry — claudecode is a local CLI with no first-party cloud variant, so this scenario is not recordable for that adapter. Forward-looking for Cursor Cloud / Codex Cloud / Copilot Cloud / Devin adapters when they exist.

claudecode.notes: agent_supports=no per coverage matrix — claudecode is local-only. Translator stops here; no recipe.

scenarios.name: session-reset
scenarios.description: Claude session runs a no-op turn, then the user invokes /clear. The old session row should disappear from the dashboard and a new session_id should appear in ready. Adapter-specific note: claudecode's /clear creates a new transcript file under the same project slug; the driver's reset_session step auto-detects the new UUID and re-points subsequent turns at it. Coverage notes a known partial caveat (#169 stale PID metadata) — if the daemon retains the old session_id past /clear, this recipe surfaces that as a fail on the old_session_ended phase.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd recording (--attach against --record daemon or isolated mode)
setup: Driver generates a fresh UUID for the first session; the reset_session step discovers the second UUID by scanning the project slug dir after /clear lands.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: fresh

verify: events.jsonl shows the FIRST UUID session entering ready, then a working/ready cycle for turn 1
after /clear, events.jsonl emits transcript_removed (and/or process_exited) for the first UUID — the 'old session disappears' assertion from the spec
events.jsonl contains a DIFFERENT UUID session appearing in ready after /clear (new session_id, fresh row)
the second turn's working/ready cycle is on the NEW UUID, not the old one
final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl contains exactly 2 user lines and 2 assistant lines (the two transcripts are concatenated by the multi-session curate path)

scenarios.name: session-resume
scenarios.description: Claude session is launched, runs one no-op turn, exits cleanly, then re-launches with the SAME --session-id and same cwd. The daemon should observe the same UUID re-appearing (not a new session row) and the second turn driving ready→working on that same session. Adapter-specific note: claudecode's resume mechanism is implicit — passing the same --session-id <UUID> at relaunch and using the same cwd causes claude to append to the existing transcript at ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<UUID>.jsonl. The driver's new `resume` step does exactly that.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
no other claudecode session uses the recipe's UUID
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID; the resume step reuses it.
Driver creates one cwd under <staging>/cwd/ and reuses it across both launches — claude caches trust per directory, so the second launch skips the trust dialog naturally.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: again

verify: events.jsonl shows the FIRST launch's pre-session entering ready, then the UUID handing off to ready, then the working/ready cycle for turn 1
events.jsonl contains a process_exited event for the UUID after turn 1 (clean exit)
events.jsonl shows a SECOND pre-session entering ready (proc-<PID2>) after the first lifetime ends
the SAME UUID session_id re-appears in events.jsonl after the resume — NOT a new UUID. This proves the session row persisted across PID lifetimes (spec: 'Same session_id reappears')
second-lifetime working transition fires on the SAME UUID, then back to ready
final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl contains exactly 2 user lines and 2 assistant lines — appended across both lifetimes since claude re-opens the existing transcript file

scenarios.name: long-idle-live-session
scenarios.description: Claude session enters ready, sits idle past readyTTL, and resumes working on the next user prompt. The recording proves irrlicht's PID-liveness check keeps the session alive past the TTL cutoff (the session would otherwise be swept on idle). Note: the production readyTTL is 30 min; this recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s on the daemon so the assertion 'session stayed past readyTTL' is testable in ~45s instead of 32min. The shortened TTL only affects the recording — production behavior is unchanged.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is running with IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s in its environment (the daemon's default 30 min would force a 32+ min recording)
irrlichd is in --record mode
no other claudecode session uses the recipe's UUID
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd (claude prompts for trust on first encounter)

Daemon's effective readyTTL must be < 30 s so the post-turn sleep can clearly exceed it. The driver script's first sleep (20 s) is calibrated to 1.33× a 15 s TTL — long enough to prove 'past TTL' without burning a full minute

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: alive

verify: events.jsonl shows the UUID session entering ready after turn 1 completes (state_transition to ready)
no transcript_removed event fires for the UUID session during the 20 s idle window (the session must not be swept while the claude process is alive)
no state_transition to working or waiting fires during the 20 s idle window (state stays ready while idle)
after the second send, events.jsonl shows ready → working → ready for the same UUID (the long-idle session resumes normally)
final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl contains exactly 2 user lines and 2 assistant lines

preconditions: codex CLI on PATH and authenticated (~/.codex/config.toml or OPENAI_API_KEY)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a --record daemon)
no other codex session uses the recipe's UUID
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/
Codex's REPL launched via drive-codex-interactive.sh stays alive across the recipe's two sends, so the daemon's process scanner observes one continuous PID across the 20 s idle window

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: alive

verify: events.jsonl shows the codex rollout session entering ready after turn 1
no transcript_removed event fires for the rollout session during the 20 s idle window
after the second send, events.jsonl shows ready → working → ready for the same rollout session
final session state is ready
codex.scope_note: Tests that codex's rollout-keyed session survives a 20 s idle window between turns. The daemon's production readyTTL is 30 min, so this recording validates 'session stays observed during idle' rather than 'past TTL' — that stricter assertion would require IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s on the daemon.

preconditions: pi CLI on PATH and authenticated (PI_API_KEY)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a --record daemon)
no concurrent pi REPL is running in the user's other terminal windows (the interactive driver assumes pi can take exclusive control)
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/
Pi's REPL launched via drive-pi-interactive.sh stays alive across the recipe's two sends

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: alive

verify: events.jsonl shows the pi UUID session entering ready after turn 1
no transcript_removed event fires for the UUID session during the 20 s idle window
after the second send, events.jsonl shows ready → working → ready for the same UUID
final session state is ready
pi.scope_note: See codex scope_note for the production readyTTL caveat.

preconditions: aider CLI on PATH and authenticated (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a --record daemon)
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (drive-aider-interactive.sh git-inits it)
Aider's TUI launched via drive-aider-interactive.sh stays alive across the recipe's two sends

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: alive

verify: events.jsonl shows aider entering ready after turn 1 (idle-flush window may delay this by up to 5 s)
no transcript_removed event fires for the .aider.chat.history.md during the 20 s idle window
after the second send, events.jsonl shows working → ready for the second turn
final session state is ready
aider.scope_note: Aider's idle-flush window (~5 s) means the post-turn ready transition lags actual LLM completion; the recipe budgets enough sleep around each turn to settle the classifier.

preconditions: opencode CLI on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd is recording with the opencode SQLite watcher enabled
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/
drive-opencode-interactive.sh issues two `opencode run --session <id>` invocations sharing the same session id, so the SQLite-backed session row spans the idle window

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: alive

verify: events.jsonl shows the opencode ses_… session entering ready after turn 1
the same ses_… id is observed across both turns (single SQLite session row, not two)
after the second send, events.jsonl shows ready → working → ready for the same ses_… id
final session state is ready
opencode.scope_note: OpenCode's process model is one `opencode run` invocation per turn (no persistent REPL); the SQLite session row chains them via --session. The daemon observes the session via SQLite polling rather than a continuous PID.

scenarios.name: session-end
scenarios.description: Three claude lifetimes recorded back-to-back, one per session-end variant from .specs/agent-scenarios.md: (1) clean exit after a no-op turn, (2) SIGKILL while idle in ready, (3) SIGKILL mid-turn while still working. The recording lets irrlicht surface three ready→working→ready arcs separated by grey gaps in the state band. Each variant tests a different teardown path and the user-observable assertion that the session disappears regardless of how it ended.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
no other claudecode session uses any of the recipe's UUIDs
setup: Driver generates 3 lowercased UUIDs: the staging-supplied one for the first session, and two via `uuidgen | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]'` for the restart steps
Driver creates 3 fresh cwds under <staging>/cwd, cwd-2, cwd-3 so claudecode shows the trust dialog for each (claude caches trust per directory)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: ok
Write a 200 word essay about the history of coffee.

verify: events.jsonl contains exactly 3 distinct UUID session_ids (one per variant) plus their proc-<PID> pre-session rows
events.jsonl contains exactly 3 process_exited events, one per UUID
variant 1: the first UUID session has a state_transition to ready right after its turn completes, immediately preceding its process_exited
variant 2: the second UUID session ends in ready (idle) when sigkill fires; no state_transition to working appears after the process_exited

variant 3: the third UUID session is in working when sigkill fires; irrlichd's sweep-promotes-to-ready behavior may or may not produce a working→ready transition before transcript_removed, depending on the 8 s tail window — accept either shape
the state band in the viewer shows three colored arcs separated by grey gaps where no session is alive between variants
transcript.jsonl contains 3 user lines and 3 assistant lines, with the variant-3 assistant message bearing stop_reason=stop_sequence (interrupted)

preconditions: codex CLI on PATH and authenticated (~/.codex/config.toml or OPENAI_API_KEY)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/
codex.prompt: Reply with exactly the word: ok
verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created and a transcript_new for the rollout session
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to ready, then working (during the turn), then ready (turn complete)
events.jsonl contains a process_exited fired AFTER the final ready (clean exit at end of headless invocation)
no state_transition to working or waiting between the final ready and process_exited
final session state is ready (before exit)
codex.scope_note: Codex's headless `codex exec` is single-shot, so only variant 1 (clean exit) is recorded. Variants 2 (SIGKILL idle) and 3 (SIGKILL mid-turn) require driver-level primitives the codex interactive driver does not currently expose.

preconditions: pi CLI on PATH and authenticated (PI_API_KEY or configured provider)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/
pi.prompt: Reply with exactly the word: ok
verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created and a transcript_new for the pi session
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to ready, then working (during the turn), then ready (turn complete)
events.jsonl contains a process_exited fired AFTER the final ready (clean exit at end of headless invocation)
no state_transition to working or waiting between the final ready and process_exited
final session state is ready (before exit)
pi.scope_note: Pi's headless `pi --print` is single-shot, so only variant 1 (clean exit) is recorded. Variants 2 (SIGKILL idle) and 3 (SIGKILL mid-turn) require driver-level primitives the pi interactive driver does not currently expose.

preconditions: aider CLI on PATH and authenticated (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/
aider.prompt: Reply with exactly the word: ok
verify: events.jsonl contains a transcript_new for .aider.chat.history.md and a state_transition to ready
events.jsonl contains a process_exited after the recording's working window closes
events.jsonl contains a transcript_removed once the aider process has gone
final session state is ready before process_exited
aider.scope_note: Aider's headless `--print` exits within ~250 ms, so only variant 1 (clean exit) is recorded. Variants 2 and 3 (SIGKILL while idle / mid-turn) require driver-level primitives the aider interactive driver does not currently expose, and aider's TUI is not designed to outlive a single --print invocation anyway.

preconditions: opencode CLI on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd is recording with the opencode SQLite watcher enabled
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created for proc-<PID> and a transcript_new for the opencode ses_… session
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to ready, then working (during the turn), then ready (turn complete)
events.jsonl contains a process_exited after the final ready (clean exit at end of `opencode run`)
no state_transition to working or waiting between the final ready and process_exited
final session state is ready (before exit)
opencode.scope_note: OpenCode's `opencode run` is single-shot per invocation, so only variant 1 (clean exit) is recorded. Variants 2 and 3 (SIGKILL idle / mid-turn) require driver-level primitives the opencode interactive driver does not currently expose.

scenarios.name: session-start
scenarios.description: Agent is launched in a fresh cwd, writes its first transcript line, sits idle. The recording proves irrlicht binds the session to ready + a PID within ~1s of agent launch. Adapter-specific note: claudecode does not create the per-session transcript file until the first user input lands — the recipe sends a 1-token nudge ('hi') so the transcript materializes and the UUID-keyed session row appears. The pre-session row (proc-<PID>) is observable from launch independent of the nudge.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
no other claudecode session uses the same UUID
setup: Generate a fresh lowercased session UUID (run-cell.sh does this via uuidgen | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (claude prompts for trust on first encounter — the driver auto-accepts)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created event for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms of the tmux new-session timestamp
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to ready for proc-<PID> within 100 ms of presession_created (pre-session immediately in ready)
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new event for the UUID session within 500 ms of the first send (claudecode creates the transcript on first input)
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to ready for the UUID session within 500 ms of transcript_new
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event for the UUID session (PID re-bound to the UUID at handoff)
events.jsonl contains a presession_removed event for proc-<PID> at handoff
final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl contains exactly 1 user line and 1 assistant line

scenarios.name: subagent-spawn
scenarios.description: Parent emits multiple Agent tool calls; daemon links each child via parent_linked. Verifies subagent transcripts land under <parent>/subagents/ and the curator bundles them into <fixture>.subagents/. This recipe doubles as the Task tool usage scenario (task #90): the Agent tool IS claudecode's Task tool, and the viewer's Tool Calls panel surfaces every invocation. For opencode the equivalent is gated on issue #277.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd recording — --attach against a --record daemon
claude session can spawn at least 3 subagents (default model has Agent tool exposed)
setup: Fresh cwd (claude's trust dialog appears once and the driver auto-accepts)
claudecode.prompt: Launch 3 Explore subagents in parallel via the Agent tool, in a single response. Subagent 1: count .go files in core/. Subagent 2: list test files in core/e2e/. Subagent 3: find imports of the 'sync' package across core/. After all three report back, reply 'done'.
verify: events.jsonl contains at least 3 parent_linked events all pointing at the same parent UUID — one per subagent the Task tool spawned
transcript.jsonl contains tool_use blocks with name=Agent — the Task tool invocations (viewer's Tool Calls panel renders these)
every parent_linked'd subagent gets its own UUID-keyed transcript file under ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<parent>/subagents/
the curator bundles the subagent transcripts into <fixture>.subagents/ so the fixture is portable
parent stays in working until the last subagent reports back, then ready
final session state is ready

scenarios.name: auto-classified-permission
scenarios.description: Claude Code running in auto mode receives a prompt that triggers a benign tool call (Bash 'echo hello' or Read on a project file) which the in-product auto-classifier approves without firing a permission prompt. The daemon must see ready→working→ready with no transition to waiting at any point. Locks down the safe-path branch of the March 2026 Auto Mode feature; the risky-path branch is owned by tool-gate-permission-prompt.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key) — must be a release that ships Auto Mode (>= 2.1.112)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
Hook installer and statusline installer in place (daemon first-run setup) so the PermissionRequest hook path is wired
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/
Driver enables auto mode (Shift+Tab until 'auto mode on' appears in the footer) before the first send

The tool call MUST be one the classifier auto-approves — a benign Bash 'echo' or a Read of a project-local file. Do NOT target .git/.claude/.vscode/.idea/.husky or network-egress shells (those flip to the risky-path scenario)

script.text: Run bash 'echo hello' using the Bash tool, then reply 'done'.

verify: events.jsonl shows the UUID session entering ready, then exactly one ready→working transition
events.jsonl contains at least one tool_use event for the Bash tool inside the working span (proves the tool call actually fired)
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to waiting for the UUID at any point (classifier auto-approved → PermissionRequest hook never fired → PermissionPending never set)
events.jsonl shows exactly one working→ready transition at turn end
final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl contains 1 user line and at least 2 assistant lines (tool_use + final reply)

scenarios.name: auto-executed-tool-call
scenarios.description: Claude executes a single auto-approved Bash tool call ('echo hello') inside one turn, then replies 'done'. Verifies the ready→working→ready arc with a tool_use mid-turn and the spec invariant that state never enters waiting for auto-executed tool calls. Distinct from full-lifecycle-toolcall, which deliberately exercises the PostToolUse hook to surface waiting; this scenario locks down the auto-approved happy path.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
Claude Code launched in auto mode or with Bash pre-approved in settings — the recipe must NOT trip a permission gate
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/
Auto mode is enabled by the driver (Shift+Tab until 'auto mode on' appears in the footer) so the safe Bash call is auto-classified

script.text: Run bash 'echo hello' using the Bash tool, then reply 'done'.

verify: events.jsonl shows pre-session entering ready, then the UUID handing off to ready
events.jsonl shows exactly one ready→working transition and one working→ready transition for the UUID
events.jsonl contains at least one tool_use event for the Bash tool inside the working span
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to waiting for the UUID during the entire run (auto-approved → PermissionPending never set)
final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl contains 1 user line and at least 2 assistant lines (tool_use + final reply)

scenarios.name: background-subagent
scenarios.description: Stub entry — claudecode's Task tool blocks the parent until the subagent reports back, so the scenario's defining shape (parent ready while named child continues working) is not producible from the current claude CLI. Forward-looking for any future claudecode background-task primitive or for adapters (OpenCode run_in_background) that already expose the capability.

claudecode.reason: Task tool blocks the parent; no SendMessage-to-background-child primitive in current claudecode CLI. irrlicht's SubagentCount(parent) also does not currently distinguish background from foreground children — both layers need work upstream before this scenario is recordable.

scenarios.name: context-compaction
scenarios.description: Claude session runs one normal turn to populate context, then the driver triggers /compact to compact the conversation history, then runs a second normal turn to prove the session continues working after compaction. Recording may be brittle: newer Claude Code (2.1.x+) writes a NEW transcript file at the compaction boundary, which can register as a fresh session row to irrlicht depending on session-stitching (see session-resume cell). Per assessment.json, irrlicht's CompactionState domain field is dead code — the dashboard cannot distinguish compaction from a normal long turn. The recipe therefore asserts only the visible state arc (ready→working→ready on each side of /compact).

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
claude version supports /compact (≥ 2.1.x)
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
/compact
Reply with exactly the word: still-here

verify: events.jsonl shows the UUID session entering ready after launch
events.jsonl shows ready→working→ready for the first turn
after /compact runs, the session settles back to ready (no stuck working, no waiting)
events.jsonl shows ready→working→ready for the post-compaction turn
final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl contains at least 2 user lines and at least 2 assistant lines (pre- and post-compaction turns); a compaction boundary marker may or may not be present depending on claude version

scenarios.name: foreground-subagent
scenarios.description: Parent claude session uses the Task (Agent) tool to launch one foreground subagent. The subagent reads a small file and reports a result; the parent waits for the subagent to finish, then replies 'done'. Verifies the parent's ready→working→ready arc wraps the subagent's lifetime, the child sidechain transcript materializes under <parent>/subagents/, and the daemon emits parent_linked binding child→parent. Predecessor recipe is the orchestrator-side subagent-spawn (3 parallel children); this scenario locks down the minimal 1-child shape per the agent-level coverage matrix.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
claude session can spawn at least 1 subagent (default model has Task/Agent tool exposed)
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (claude's trust dialog appears once and the driver auto-accepts)
A README.md with a single H1 heading is seeded into the cwd so the subagent has a deterministic file to read

script.text: Use the Task tool to launch a general-purpose subagent that reads the README.md in the current directory and reports the first heading. Wait for the subagent to finish, then reply 'done'.

verify: events.jsonl shows the parent UUID entering ready, then a single ready→working transition driven by the user prompt
events.jsonl contains at least 1 parent_linked event pointing at the parent UUID — the child sidechain transcript was discovered and bound
transcript.jsonl contains a tool_use block with name=Agent (the Task tool invocation)
the subagent transcript is present under ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<parent>/subagents/agent-<uuid>.jsonl
no state_transition to ready or waiting fires on the parent UUID between parent_linked and the final working→ready (parent stays working until the child reports back)
events.jsonl shows exactly one working→ready transition for the parent UUID at turn end
final session state for the parent is ready

scenarios.name: long-agentic-session-stress
scenarios.description: Compressed proxy for a real long session: 4 plain user prompts in one session. Spec asserts per-turn ready→working→ready arcs with no flicker WITHIN a turn, no stuck state. Tool calls were originally part of the recipe but the modern claudecode default (Sonnet 4.6 + high effort) refuses auto-mode for paid models, which stalls tool-call turns at the permission gate. Plain prompts keep the recording deterministic; the no-flicker-within-turn invariant is still meaningful because each turn still has multiple intermediate assistant chunks even without tool_use.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)

Claude Code launched in auto mode (Read/Bash/Write pre-approved) so no turn trips a permission gate — otherwise state would legitimately enter waiting and the no-flicker invariant would need to allow it
cwd under <staging>/cwd/ contains a CHANGELOG.md file (driver writes a stub if not present)
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/
Driver seeds CHANGELOG.md in cwd with a small fixture (e.g. '## 0.4.0 - 2026-05-01\n- initial') so turn 1's Read finds something
Auto mode is enabled by the driver (Shift+Tab until 'auto mode on' appears in the footer) so Bash/Read/Write are auto-classified

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: alpha
Reply with exactly the word: beta
Reply with exactly the word: gamma
Reply with exactly the word: delta

verify: events.jsonl shows the UUID session entering ready after launch
for EACH of the 4 turns: exactly one ready→working transition, at least one tool_use event inside the working span, exactly one working→ready transition at turn end
across the entire session, NO state_transition to ready fires inside any turn's working span (no flicker between failing/succeeding tool_use and the next tool_use inside the same turn)
across the entire session, NO state_transition to waiting fires (auto mode means no permission gate)
final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl contains exactly 4 user lines and at least 8 assistant lines (tool_use + final reply per turn)

scenarios.name: mid-turn-message-queued
scenarios.description: Claude is given a slow count-to-10 prompt; while the agent is mid-turn, the driver types a follow-up message into the TUI which claudecode queues until a safe point. The daemon must see a single contiguous working span with no flicker to ready when the queued input lands, and the cycle must still end at ready once everything is consumed. Locks down the parser's transcript-driven state classification against a future regression where queued text appears as a synthetic user-line before consumption.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
Claude Code launched in auto mode so neither prompt trips a permission gate
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/
The first prompt must keep claude busy long enough (a few seconds) to type a follow-up into the TUI — a slow count-to-10 with explicit pauses is sufficient
The mid-turn follow-up is delivered via tmux send-keys ending in Enter; claudecode's TUI buffers the text and consumes it at the next safe point (post-tool boundary or turn end)

script.text: Count from 1 to 10, one number per line, with a brief pause between each. Then on the last line reply with exactly the word: done

script.keys: f o l l o w u p Space q u e u e d Enter

verify: events.jsonl shows the UUID entering ready, then a ready→working transition for the first prompt
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to ready between the first ready→working and the queued-message consumption (the working span must be contiguous through the queued arrival)
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to waiting at any point during the working span
events.jsonl eventually shows a working→ready transition; final session state is ready

transcript.jsonl contains at least 1 user line for the initial prompt; the queued follow-up may appear as a second user line (consumed as a new turn) or be folded into the first turn — either shape is acceptable per spec

scenarios.name: model-identification
scenarios.description: Single no-op turn in a claudecode session launched with a non-default model (e.g. --model claude-opus-4-7). Locks down that extractClaudeCodeModel pulls the model name from raw.message.model on the first assistant event and that SessionMetrics.ModelName is populated (not 'unknown') and ContextWindow matches the model's published window (via raw.context_management.context_window when present, the [1m] suffix heuristic, or the LiteLLM alias-table fallback). The state arc is the basic-turn arc; the assertion lives in events.jsonl payload.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
no other claudecode session uses the recipe's UUID
the chosen non-default model name is available on the maintainer's account (e.g. claude-opus-4-7 or claude-sonnet-4-5)
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/
Driver launches claude with --model <non-default-model> so the first assistant event's message.model field carries that name from turn 1
Assertions on ModelName/ContextWindow are checked AFTER turn_end — ContextWindow may briefly read 0 between session detection and the first assistant event (per assessment caveat)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl shows one ready→working→ready cycle on the UUID
events.jsonl carries a model_name on the post-turn state_transition / metric snapshot equal to the normalized launch model (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-7' or 'claude-sonnet-4-5'), NOT 'unknown'
SessionMetrics.ContextWindow after turn_end matches the launch model's published window (1_000_000 for [1m] flavours; per-model default from the alias table otherwise)
SessionMetrics.ContextWindowUnknown is false after turn_end (unless the alias table is lagging — see assessment caveat 2)
transcript.jsonl contains 1 user line and ≥ 1 assistant line
final session state is ready

scenarios.name: model-switch-midsession
scenarios.description: Two no-op turns in a single claudecode session separated by a /model slash command that swaps the active model. Locks down (a) the parser's per-turn Contribution.Model attribution (turn 1 → model A, turn 2 → model B), (b) MergeMetrics' latest-non-unknown-wins rule promoting ModelName to model B by t2_end, and (c) the isLocalCommandContent filter dropping the /model wrapper so no state machinery fires during the switch window (no working/waiting flicker). TotalTokens accumulates across the two models (T2 > T1). NOTE: claudecode's /model picker is interactive; the recipe drives the TUI via send + keys + sleep. If the picker UX is unreliable across claude versions, the recipe author may need to extend the driver with model-picker-specific support (e.g. a dedicated model_switch step).

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
no other claudecode session uses the recipe's UUID
both model A (launch model, e.g. claude-sonnet-4-5) and model B (switch target, e.g. claude-opus-4-7) are available on the maintainer's account
the running claude CLI version supports the /model slash command with arrow-key selection in the picker (confirmed in 2.1.x)
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/
Driver launches claude with --model <model-A> so turn 1 is attributed to A
Between turns the driver opens /model, selects model B with Down+Enter (or up arrow depending on picker order), and waits for the picker to dismiss before the next send
Compare ModelName against the NORMALIZED form (lowercased, provider-prefix stripped) — not the typed form (assessment caveat 2)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: turn-one
/model
Reply with exactly the word: turn-two

verify: events.jsonl shows two ready→working→ready cycles on the same UUID (one per turn)
events.jsonl carries model_name = <model A normalized> on the t1_end state_transition / metric snapshot
events.jsonl carries model_name = <model B normalized> on the t2_end state_transition / metric snapshot (latest-wins)
events.jsonl shows NO state_transition to working or waiting during the /model picker window between t1_end and t2_start (the <local-command> wrappers are filtered by isLocalCommandContent)
PerTurnContribution.Model for turn 1 = model A; for turn 2 = model B (per-turn cost attribution)
SessionMetrics.TotalTokens after t2_end > TotalTokens after t1_end (accumulates across both models)
transcript.jsonl contains exactly 2 user lines and ≥ 2 assistant lines (the /model slash itself does NOT appear as a user line)
final session state is ready

scenarios.name: multiple-agents-same-workspace
scenarios.description: Stub entry — capability is supported by all adapters and irrlicht (per-adapter ProcessMatcher, disjoint transcript roots, per-session metrics keyed by (adapter, session_id)). Recording is blocked on the driver: current ir:onboard-agent orchestrates one CLI at a time and has no cross-adapter parallel mode. Becomes recordable once the driver gains a paired-adapter step type that can drive `claude` and `codex` (or `claude` + `pi`) concurrently in the same cwd.

claudecode.reason: requires a second agent CLI (codex/pi) running in parallel; current driver orchestrates one CLI at a time. The matrix pairs this cell to both claudecode and codex adapters — once the cross-adapter driver exists, a single recording satisfies both rows.

scenarios.name: multiple-sessions-same-cwd
scenarios.description: Stub entry — capability is supported by both claudecode and irrlicht (assessment.json confidence 0.9), but the current ir:onboard-agent driver supports one tmux session at a time and cannot orchestrate two concurrent `claude` processes in the same cwd. Becomes recordable once the driver gains a parallel_session / tmux_split step type that can route `send` and `wait_turn` to named targets.

claudecode.reason: current driver supports one tmux session at a time; multi-pane orchestration of two concurrent `claude` processes in the same cwd needs a driver extension (named-target send/wait_turn). Agent and irrlicht both support the capability as-shipped; only the recording rig is missing.

scenarios.name: oversized-transcript-line
scenarios.description: Claude is prompted to emit a single very long sentence (≥5000 words, no embedded newlines) so the transcript flushes one large JSONL line. Recording may be brittle: 5000 words is moderate (~30 KB) — well under irrlicht's 64 MB tailer cap (issue #270) and also under bufio.Scanner's old 2 MB limit, so the test is a sanity-check rather than a true cap-stress. The spec asserts only the visible state arc (ready→working→ready); the absence of a parser stall is INFERRED from the arc completing. Per assessment.json caveats, manufacturing a truly oversized (>2 MB) line from a real claude CLI is awkward — a follow-up cell can hand-craft a synthetic >2 MB line into the live transcript file if deeper cap-stress coverage is needed.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/

script.text: Write a single very long sentence on one line. Make it at least 5000 words. Do not use newlines except at the end.

verify: events.jsonl shows the UUID session entering ready after launch
events.jsonl shows exactly one ready→working transition for the UUID
events.jsonl shows exactly one working→ready transition at turn end (completion of the arc is the parser-resilience proof; if the tailer wedged on the long line this transition would never fire)
irrlichd logs do NOT contain a 'skipping oversized line' warning for the UUID's transcript (a 5000-word line is well under the 64 MB cap)
final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl contains 1 user line and at least 1 assistant line; the assistant line is a single long JSONL row with no embedded newlines in message.content

scenarios.name: self-correction-iteration
scenarios.description: Claude runs bash 'false' (which fails), inspects the failing tool_result, retries with bash 'echo recovered', then replies 'done'. The whole arc must show a single contiguous working span — no flicker to ready or waiting between the failed attempt and the recovery. Locks down the parser's terminal-stop_reason gate (intermediate tool_use/tool_result pairs do not change state).

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
Claude Code launched in auto mode (Bash auto-approved) so neither attempt trips a permission gate
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/
Auto mode is enabled by the driver (Shift+Tab until 'auto mode on' appears in the footer)

script.text: Run bash 'false' first; if it fails, retry with bash 'echo recovered'. Then reply 'done'.

verify: events.jsonl shows exactly one ready→working transition for the UUID
events.jsonl contains at least 2 tool_use events for the Bash tool inside the working span (the failing attempt and the recovery)
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to ready between the failing tool_result and the recovery tool_use
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to waiting at any point during the working span
events.jsonl shows exactly one working→ready transition at turn end
final session state is ready

scenarios.name: shell-escape-command
scenarios.description: Driver types Claude Code's !cmd shell-escape (`!echo hello`) in the interactive REPL. claude runs the shell command locally without an LLM round-trip and writes <bash-input>/<bash-stdout> wrapper events to the transcript. Per parser.go:216-225, irrlicht's claudecode parser deliberately skips these wrapper events, so state must stay in ready throughout (no synthesized turn). The recipe then sends a normal user prompt to prove the session is still alive and a real prompt still drives the ready→working→ready arc. Verdict adopts the intent reading of the spec (no stuck in working) per assessment.json — the alternative literal reading would require a parser change to synthesize a transition arc.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
claude is launched in interactive TUI mode so the ! shell-escape syntax is available
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/

script.keys: ! e c h o Space h e l l o Enter

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: alive

verify: events.jsonl shows the UUID session entering ready after launch
during the ~5 s window after the !echo shell-escape, NO state_transition to working fires for the UUID (the parser must skip <bash-input>/<bash-stdout> wrappers)
during that window, NO state_transition to waiting fires
after the normal user prompt, events.jsonl shows ready→working→ready for the UUID
final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl contains the shell-escape wrapper lines (<bash-input> / <bash-stdout>) plus 1 user line and at least 1 assistant line for the final turn

scenarios.name: streaming-partial-writes
scenarios.description: Claude streams a multi-sentence prose response across several JSONL chunks. The parser must keep the session in working across all intermediate assistant_streaming events (non-terminal stop_reason) and only flip to ready when the terminal end_turn event lands. This locks down the Bug-D streaming-flicker family (issue #102): resolveAssistantStreaming (parser.go:288) downgrades non-terminal stop_reasons to assistant_streaming, which is excluded from IsAgentDone(). The streaming_window phase pins the no-flicker invariant.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/

script.text: Write a 5-sentence story about a robot. Each sentence on its own line.

verify: events.jsonl shows the UUID session entering ready after launch
events.jsonl shows exactly one ready→working transition for the UUID
across the streaming window (multiple assistant_streaming events with non-terminal stop_reason), NO state_transition to ready fires before the terminal end_turn line
NO state_transition to waiting fires during the streaming window
events.jsonl shows exactly one working→ready transition at turn end
final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl contains 1 user line and at least 2 assistant lines (intermediate streaming chunks + terminal)

scenarios.name: subagent-orphan-cleanup
scenarios.description: Parent claude session launches a foreground subagent via the Task tool; once the parent_linked event fires and the child sidechain transcript is visible, the recipe SIGKILLs the parent's process group, leaving the child orphaned. The daemon's pid_manager sweep must emit process_exited for both parent and child without a spurious state_transition to working for the orphaned child during cleanup. Regression coverage for issue #321 (zombie-on-restart). The spec describes a 5-child variant; this recipe records the minimal 1-child shape since the no-working-transition invariant is identical for any N≥1.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
the sigkill driver step kills the entire claude process group (not just the parent PID) so the child is left orphaned rather than reaped by its own clean exit
claude session can spawn at least 1 subagent (default model has Task/Agent tool exposed)
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/
A README.md is seeded so the subagent has something to read while the parent is alive — the subagent must still be in flight when SIGKILL lands

script.text: Use the Task tool to launch a general-purpose subagent that reads README.md three times in a row and then waits 30 seconds before reporting back. Do not reply to me until the subagent is done.

verify: events.jsonl contains a parent_linked event for the child UUID pointing at the parent UUID — the child transcript was discovered before SIGKILL
events.jsonl contains exactly one process_exited event for the parent UUID
events.jsonl contains exactly one process_exited event for the child UUID
no state_transition to working fires for the child UUID after the parent's process_exited (issue #321 regression invariant)
no state_transition to waiting fires for the child UUID after the parent's process_exited
after readyTTL, both rows are removed via transcript_removed (cleanup-to-removal window may be ~30s depending on readyTTL)
transcript.jsonl contains the parent's user line plus at least one assistant tool_use=Agent, and the child sidechain transcript under <parent>/subagents/ is bundled into <fixture>.subagents/

scenarios.name: synchronous-slash-command
scenarios.description: Claude session receives two synchronous slash commands (/cost and /help) that do not call the model, then a tiny normal prompt to prove the session is still alive. State must stay ready throughout the slash-command window — the parser's isLocalCommandContent filter drops the <local-command> wrappers so no state machinery fires. Locks down the regression where a future TUI change logs slash invocations as real user messages.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
The slash commands chosen (/cost, /help) must remain local-only in the running claude CLI version — if Anthropic ever routes /cost through the model the scenario premise breaks
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/
Avoid /clear (mints a fresh session UUID — different scenario) and /compact, /init (real model calls — different scenarios)

script.text: Reply with exactly: warm

verify: events.jsonl shows the UUID session entering ready at session birth and staying ready throughout the /cost + /help window (no state_transition to working fires until the final 'Reply with...' send)
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to waiting at any point during the slash-command window
events.jsonl shows exactly one ready→working→ready cycle for the entire run — driven by the final normal turn only
transcript.jsonl contains exactly 1 user line and 1 assistant line (the slash commands do NOT appear as user turns; their <local-command> wrappers are filtered by the parser)
final session state is ready

scenarios.name: token-accounting
scenarios.description: Three back-to-back no-op turns ('Reply with the word: ok') in a single claudecode session. Exercises the per-turn UsageBreakdown extraction (input/output/cache tokens from raw.message.usage) and the monotonic accumulation into SessionMetrics.TotalTokens across multiple requestId boundaries. The state arc is unremarkable (three ready→working→ready cycles); the assertion lives in events.jsonl payload — each turn boundary emits a PerTurnContribution and TotalTokens is non-decreasing turn-over-turn. Token & cost extraction is already covered by basic-turn for a single turn; this recipe locks down the multi-turn accumulator.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
no other claudecode session uses the recipe's UUID
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/
No special model or context flags — default model is fine; the assertion is about token accumulation, not which model produced the tokens

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl shows three ready→working→ready cycles on the same UUID (one per turn)
events.jsonl carries a PerTurnContribution / UsageBreakdown for each of the three turns; input_tokens + output_tokens > 0 on every turn
SessionMetrics.TotalTokens after turn 1 > 0
SessionMetrics.TotalTokens after turn 2 ≥ TotalTokens after turn 1 (monotonic)
SessionMetrics.TotalTokens after turn 3 ≥ TotalTokens after turn 2 (monotonic across 3 turns)
transcript.jsonl contains exactly 3 user lines and ≥ 3 assistant lines
final session state is ready

scenarios.name: token-quota-exhausted
scenarios.description: Mocked 429 recording: a local Anthropic-compatible stub (tools/mock-anthropic-429.go) returns 200 + end_turn on the first /v1/messages request and HTTP 429 on the second. claude runs under --bare so it reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL from env (OAuth/keychain are bypassed). The bespoke recorder at tools/record-token-quota-exhausted.sh handles launch, driving, and fixture staging — the standard driver-claudecode-interactive.sh does not pass --bare.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH
go on PATH (to build the mock)
irrlichd running with --record (recording is captured via attached daemon)
port 18765 available on localhost
setup: tools/record-token-quota-exhausted.sh builds the mock, launches it, drives claude under --bare, and stages the fixture under .build/refresh/claudecode/token-quota-exhausted-<TS>/.
verify: events.jsonl shows session_birth → prompt1 working/ready arc → prompt2 working/ready arc
final state of the session is ready
no state_transition to waiting (no permission gates expected)
the second prompt's working→ready transition is the quota-exhausted code path

scenarios.name: tool-gate-permission-prompt
scenarios.description: Claude invokes the Bash tool; a PreToolUse hook returns permissionDecision=ask, deterministically opening the permission prompt. Validates the rule-0 (PermissionPending=true → waiting) path in state_classifier.go, which is the most authoritative gate and predates HasOpenToolCall — defends against the race fixed in #108 where the hook fires before the JSONL tool_use line lands. Recipe captures entry into waiting and stops; the second-scenario variant (late PostToolUse after ready) is a no-op in the classifier and not exercised here.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
irrlicht's Claude hook installer has run (hookinstaller.go) so PermissionRequest / PostToolUse hooks route to the daemon
no other claudecode session uses the same UUID
setup: Generate a fresh lowercased session UUID
Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (claude prompts for trust on first encounter — the driver auto-accepts)
Project-local settings.json includes the PreToolUse 'ask' hook below so Bash invocations open the permission prompt deterministically (no reliance on claude's allowlist heuristics)

script.text: Run bash 'echo hello' using the Bash tool.

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new for the UUID session within 500 ms of the first send
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to working for the UUID after the prompt lands
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to waiting for the UUID once the PreToolUse hook fires (PermissionPending=true → rule 0)
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to ready for the UUID after the waiting transition (recipe terminates while session is in waiting)
final session state is waiting
transcript.jsonl contains 1 user line and at least 1 assistant line; one assistant message has a tool_use with name=Bash that is gated by the permission prompt

scenarios.name: turn-aborted-by-error
scenarios.description: Agent's model call fails mid-turn (network error, provider 5xx, timeout). No normal turn-end signal is emitted. Expected arc per spec: ready → working, then → ready within the sweep interval after the agent gives up. Failures split into three buckets: Bucket A (transcript epilogue with synthesized error text → observable via classifier rule 2b — CAPTURED via tools/mock-anthropic-5xx.go), Bucket B (pure mid-stream network failure with NO transcript write → currently UNOBSERVABLE because there is no inactivity sweep on the working state since PR #106 removed the stale-tool timer), Bucket C (system/turn_duration on abnormal exit → observable via turn_done — not yet captured separately).

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH
go on PATH (to build the mock)
irrlichd running with --record (recording is captured via attached daemon)
port 18766 available on localhost
setup: tools/record-turn-aborted-by-error.sh builds tools/mock-anthropic-5xx.go, launches it, drives claude under --bare against the mock, and stages the fixture under .build/refresh/claudecode/turn-aborted-by-error-<TS>/. The mock streams message_start + a partial content_block_delta then emits SSE `event: error` (overloaded_error); claudecode appends a synthesized 'API Error' assistant message with a terminal stop_reason and IsAgentDone() fires.
verify: events.jsonl shows session_birth → ready
events.jsonl shows pid_discovered within 1000 ms of birth
events.jsonl shows ready → working when the user prompt lands
events.jsonl shows working → ready after the mock's mid-stream error → synthesized assistant epilogue with terminal stop_reason (Bucket A path)
session state does NOT stick in working — recovery is event-driven (not via sweep, which doesn't exist today)

Bucket B (silent network drop with no transcript epilogue) and Bucket C (system/turn_duration on abnormal exit) are intentionally NOT exercised by this recipe — they remain documented gaps in assessment.json

scenarios.name: turn-end-terminal-text
scenarios.description: Smallest tool-less round-trip pinning the terminal-text end signal: the assistant produces a final message with stop_reason: end_turn and no tool_use, and the classifier drives working→ready via rule 2b. Overlaps intentionally with basic-turn — this cell focuses on the end-signal mechanism (parser.resolveAssistantStreaming's terminal stop_reason allow-list, the Bug-D fix from issue #102), rather than the broader happy-path lifecycle.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
no other claudecode session uses the same UUID
setup: Generate a fresh lowercased session UUID (run-cell.sh does this via uuidgen | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (claude prompts for trust on first encounter — the driver auto-accepts)

script.text: Reply with exactly: hello world

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new for the UUID session within 500 ms of the first send
events.jsonl contains exactly one state_transition to working and exactly one state_transition to ready for the UUID
events.jsonl contains NO tool_use events for the UUID
transcript.jsonl's final assistant message has stop_reason=end_turn
final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl contains exactly 1 user line and 1 assistant line

scenarios.name: user-blocking-plan-mode-approval
scenarios.description: Claude is started in plan mode via permissions.defaultMode=plan, then prompted to make a plan; the agent emits an ExitPlanMode tool_use and pauses for approval. Validates the same waiting path as user-blocking-question but via the ExitPlanMode branch (tailer_config.go user-blocking tool list, hooks.go toolExitPlanMode case). Recipe captures only entry into waiting; the approve/reject continuation is structurally identical to other waiting-resume paths.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
claude --settings honours permissions.defaultMode (verified in 2.1.x)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
no other claudecode session uses the same UUID
setup: Generate a fresh lowercased session UUID
Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (claude prompts for trust on first encounter — the driver auto-accepts)

script.text: You are in plan mode. ExitPlanMode is how you PROPOSE a plan BEFORE doing any work — you do NOT need to (and must not) read, write, or explore anything first. Call ExitPlanMode immediately as your first and only tool call. Pass this exact plan in the `plan` field: 'Add a new file hello.py with a single line: print("hello world").' No other text, no other tools.

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new for the UUID session within 500 ms of the first send
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to working for the UUID after the prompt lands
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to waiting for the UUID once the assistant emits the ExitPlanMode tool_use
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to ready for the UUID after the waiting transition (recipe terminates while session is in waiting)
final session state is waiting
transcript.jsonl contains 1 user line and at least 1 assistant line; one assistant message has a tool_use with name=ExitPlanMode

scenarios.name: user-blocking-question
scenarios.description: Agent's reply ends in a question mark, signalling it's waiting for user input. Validates the trailing-? path through the state classifier (session.IsWaitingForUserInput → state_classifier.go decision 2a). The recipe deliberately stops at the waiting episode and does not answer the question — locking down the entry into waiting. The coalesced-flush second-scenario variant (#150) is covered by unit tests around ShouldSynthesizeCollapsedWaiting.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
no other claudecode session uses the same UUID
setup: Generate a fresh lowercased session UUID (run-cell.sh does this via uuidgen | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (claude prompts for trust on first encounter — the driver auto-accepts)

script.text: Reply with exactly one short clarifying question, ending in a question mark. No other text.

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new for the UUID session within 500 ms of the first send
events.jsonl contains exactly one state_transition to working for the UUID
events.jsonl contains exactly one state_transition to waiting for the UUID after the assistant's question-mark reply
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to ready for the UUID after the waiting transition (recipe terminates while session is in waiting)
final session state is waiting
transcript.jsonl contains exactly 1 user line and 1 assistant line, with the assistant text ending in '?'

scenarios.name: user-esc-interrupt
scenarios.description: User cancels a long turn with ESC. Validates parser.recordUserInterruptFlags (sets ev.IsUserInterrupt on '[Request interrupted by user]' marker), the sticky LastWasUserInterrupt flag in tailer.go, and classifier rule 3 driving working→ready with reason 'user ESC interrupt'. Spec: ready→working→ready; state does not stick in working post-interrupt. The driver's 'interrupt' step sends Escape to the tmux pane.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
claude version where ESC interrupts the in-flight turn (confirmed in 2.1.x)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
no other claudecode session uses the same UUID
setup: Generate a fresh lowercased session UUID
Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (claude prompts for trust on first encounter — the driver auto-accepts)

script.text: Count slowly from 1 to 100, with a brief one-sentence explanation for each number. Take your time and stream the response.

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new for the UUID session within 500 ms of the first send
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to working for the UUID after the prompt lands
transcript.jsonl contains a user message whose first content text begins with '[Request interrupted by user]' (and NOT 'for tool use')
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to ready for the UUID after the interrupt marker is parsed (rule 3 fires on LastWasUserInterrupt + LastEventType=user + !HasOpenToolCall)
final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl contains exactly 1 user prompt line, 1 interrupt-marker user line, and at least 1 partial assistant line

scenarios.name: autonomous-loop
scenarios.description: Claudecode /goal autonomous self-prompting loop. /goal <condition> registers a Stop hook that re-prompts with the condition after each turn ends; the agent iterates until goal_status.met == true or /goal clear fires. The recording uses a goal that converges in 2-3 iterations.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd is recording (--attach or isolated mode)
/goal requires a trusted workspace and hooks enabled (default settings satisfy both)
setup: Driver generates a fresh UUID + cwd; trust dialog is accepted automatically

script.text: /goal You have run two bash commands so far this session: `echo iter1` and `echo iter2`, in that exact order. Run them one at a time across iterations.
/goal clear

verify: events.jsonl contains at least 2 working→ready transitions on the same UUID — the /goal set + at least one Stop-hook iteration
final state of the session is ready after /goal clear
no state_transition to waiting (no permission gates expected)

scenarios.name: autonomous-loop-iteration-limit
scenarios.description: Claudecode /goal with an unsatisfiable condition; the recipe lets the loop run a few iterations then sends /goal clear as a stand-in for the max_iterations cap (which is not deterministically forceable today). The state arc on cap-hit and on /goal clear is identical: end_turn followed by ready with no further Stop-hook re-prompts. Tests the loop's graceful-termination shape from the spec.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd is recording
/goal requires a trusted workspace and hooks enabled
setup: Driver generates a fresh UUID + cwd

script.text: /goal You have replied with the integer that is both odd and even simultaneously. This goal is impossible. Keep guessing.
/goal clear

verify: events.jsonl contains at least 2 working->ready transitions on the same UUID
final state of the session is ready after /goal clear
no state_transition to waiting

scenarios.name: task-tool
scenarios.description: Staggered Task-tool fan-out: parent kicks off three concurrent subagents in one turn, each running bash sleep N (5/10/15s). The recording shows the dashboard subagent count decrement 3 → 2 → 1 → 0 over ~15s, then the parent transitions working → ready after the last child reports. Generalizes foreground-subagent (1 child) and the prior task-tool recipe (2 children) to the three-stagger shape that exercises the dashboard count chip end-to-end.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd is recording (--attach or isolated)
claude session can spawn at least 2 subagents in parallel (default model has Task/Agent tool exposed)
setup: Driver seeds two files in <staging>/cwd: README.md (one H1 heading) and CHANGELOG.md (one release date). Subagents have deterministic content to read.

script.text: In a SINGLE turn, use the Task tool THREE times in parallel. Each subagent receives the general-purpose type. Subagent A: run bash 'sleep 5 && echo A done'. Subagent B: run bash 'sleep 10 && echo B done'. Subagent C: run bash 'sleep 15 && echo C done'. After all three subagents report back, reply 'done'.

verify: events.jsonl shows the parent UUID entering ready, then a single ready->working transition
events.jsonl contains AT LEAST 2 parent_linked events pointing at the parent UUID
transcript.jsonl contains AT LEAST 2 tool_use blocks with name=Agent (the two Task invocations)
two subagent transcripts appear under ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<parent>/subagents/agent-<uuid>.jsonl
no state_transition to ready or waiting fires on the parent UUID between child2_parent_linked and the final working->ready (parent stays working until the LAST child reports back)
events.jsonl shows exactly one working->ready transition for the parent UUID at turn end
final session state for the parent is ready

preconditions: opencode CLI on PATH and authenticated (run a single message in opencode TUI to confirm)
irrlichd is recording (--attach or isolated) with the opencode SQLite watcher enabled
the default opencode model exposes the `task` tool (most chat models do; lightweight 'codex' models may not)
setup: Driver seeds two files in <staging>/cwd: README.md (one H1 heading) and CHANGELOG.md (one release date). Subagents have deterministic content to read.

Driver runs `opencode` in tmux against <staging>/cwd so each scenario gets a fresh project directory. OpenCode appends to the global ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db; sessions are isolated by their session id, not by cwd.

script.text: In a SINGLE turn, use the `task` tool THREE times in parallel. Subagent A: run bash 'sleep 5 && echo A done'. Subagent B: run bash 'sleep 10 && echo B done'. Subagent C: run bash 'sleep 15 && echo C done'. After all three subagents report back, reply 'done'.

verify: events.jsonl shows the parent session entering ready, then a single ready->working transition
events.jsonl contains AT LEAST 2 parent_linked events pointing at the parent session id
the parent session's transcript (SQLite parts) contains AT LEAST 2 tool parts with tool=task
two or more child session rows appear in ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db with parent_id = parent session id
no state_transition to ready or waiting fires on the parent between child2_parent_linked and the final working->ready (parent stays working until the LAST child reports back)
events.jsonl shows exactly one working->ready transition for the parent at turn end
final session state for the parent is ready

orchestrator_scenarios.name: agent-discovery
orchestrator_scenarios.description: Two rigs declared in rigs.json; gt rig list returns the same set. Verifies that both rigs surface as codebases with their default workers (witness, refinery), and that mayor/deacon appear as idle global agents when no sessions match.

orchestrator_scenarios.name: role-assignment
orchestrator_scenarios.description: Two sessions are active with CWDs under <rig>/witness and <rig>/refinery. The poller must populate worker.SessionID for those roles via deriveRole/CWD matching.

orchestrator_scenarios.name: state-aggregation
orchestrator_scenarios.description: Daemon reports running=false and the rig is degraded. Verifies that orchestrator.State.Running and Codebase.Status reflect aggregated multi-source state.

orchestrator_scenarios.name: agent-join-leave
orchestrator_scenarios.description: Polecat workforce churns over 3 polls: tick-001 has 1 polecat, tick-002 adds a second, tick-003 removes the second. Verifies that the snapshot stream reflects each join/leave on its own poll cycle.

orchestrator_scenarios.name: polling-lifecycle
orchestrator_scenarios.description: gt CLI is healthy on tick 1, fails completely on tick 2 (poller falls back to cached rigs.json sidecar; polecats/dogs/boot become empty since they have no fallback), then recovers on tick 3. Verifies the production fallback path through the rigs.json sidecar.
